title: Refactoring Challenge
subtitle: Apply the Factory pattern yourself. Fix the bad code.

challenges.title: Extract a Simple Factory
challenges.description: This NotificationService creates objects directly with a massive switch statement. Extract a NotificationFactory class so the service only calls factory.create(type).
hints: Create a new class called NotificationFactory
Move the switch statement into a static create() method
The service should only have: Notification n = NotificationFactory.create(type)

challenges.title: Upgrade Switch to Registry
challenges.description: The Simple Factory works but still has a switch statement. Convert it to a Registry pattern using Map<String, Supplier<Notification>> to eliminate all if-else logic.
hints: Use a Map<String, Supplier<Notification>> as the registry
Initialize it in a static block or static method
The create() method becomes a single map.get() + null check
Add a public register() method for extensibility
validationChecks: Map or HashMap is used for registry
register() method exists and is public
No switch or if-else in create()

challenges.title: Design an Abstract Factory
challenges.description: A payment system processes orders for India and US regions. Each region needs its OWN tax calculator, receipt generator, and currency formatter that must be used TOGETHER. Design the Abstract Factory.
hints: Identify the FAMILY: TaxCalculator + ReceiptGenerator + CurrencyFormatter
Create an interface RegionalComplianceFactory with three create methods
IndiaComplianceFactory returns GST + Indian receipt + Rupee formatter
USComplianceFactory returns US tax + US receipt + Dollar formatter
The client only depends on the abstract factory interface
